title: Junior Scrum Master (w/m/d)
required_skills: Minimum 2 years experience as Scrum Master or comparable agile role
Ability to train and coach teams in agile methods
Solid understanding of software development processes and cross-functional collaboration tools
Ability to make agile processes deliver measurable results
Strong communication and constructive collaboration approach
bonus_skills: Agile method optimisation and continuous improvement
matched_skills: Agile sprint ceremonies: Hapag-Lloyd — participated in sprint planning, stand-ups, retrospectives as coordination point
Cross-functional coordination in agile environment: Hapag-Lloyd cross-team delivery coordination
KPI development for process improvement: VfB Stuttgart KPI framework; Hapag-Lloyd performance reporting
Team facilitation: HSBC town hall facilitation, sprint review coordination
Agile delivery stakeholder communication: Hapag-Lloyd executive sprint review reports
missing_skills: 2 years formal Scrum Master role (Hapag-Lloyd role was coordination, not Scrum Master formally)
Impediment removal and coaching capability as primary responsibility
core_responsibilities: Introduce, develop, and continuously optimise agile methods and processes
Coach teams in agile working methods and support self-organisation
Organise and moderate all Scrum events (planning, reviews, retrospectives)
Develop KPIs, analyse processes, and support continuous improvement
Foster culture of openness and development
recommended_emphasis: Hapag-Lloyd sprint ceremony participation (planning, stand-ups, retrospectives) as direct Scrum practice evidence
VfB Stuttgart KPI framework design as process improvement and measurement capability
HSBC meeting facilitation and cross-workstream coordination as facilitation experience
Hapag-Lloyd agile delivery coordination — position as proto-Scrum Master experience
Confluence/JIRA tooling as agile team toolset familiarity
summary: Score 62.5 (moderate match). Boru has genuine agile coordination experience from Hapag-Lloyd (sprint ceremonies, cross-functional delivery coordination) that overlaps significantly with junior Scrum Master responsibilities. The gap is lack of formal Scrum Master title/certification and explicit coaching/impediment removal experience. This is a viable application — the 'junior' designation accommodates candidates building toward the Scrum Master role. Recommend getting a PSM-I certification to strengthen candidacy.
